pattern: Bit manipulation: count the 1-bits in n that differ from k, checking impossibility when k has a 1-bit that n does not.
hints: You can only turn 1-bits in n into 0-bits — you can never add a 1-bit.
If k has a bit set that n does not, the task is impossible — return -1.
Otherwise, count each bit position where n has 1 but k has 0; that count is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: n equals k — zero changes needed
k has a bit not in n — impossible
n has all bits k has plus more — clear all extras
k is a superset of n's bits — impossible even though they look close
n and k differ only by one bit in n — one change
Both values near max 1000000, n is a superset of k bits

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    k = rng.randint(1, size)
    return [n, k]
